mission.title: Install and Configure Open WebUI
mission.description: Open WebUI is a user-friendly AI interface designed for self-hosting various AI models, including Ollama and OpenAI APIs. It provides a seamless setup experience on Kubernetes, making it ideal for developers looking to deploy AI applications efficiently. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and resource allocation for optimal performance.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the necessary command-line tools to interact with your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

curl -LO https://storage.googleapis.com/kubernetes-release/release/$(curl -s https://storage.googleapis.com/kubernetes-release/release/stable.txt)/bin/linux/amd64/kubectl

chmod +x ./kubectl

sudo mv ./kubectl /usr/local/bin/kubectl

kubectl version --client

```

> Download the latest kubectl binary.
> Make the kubectl binary executable.
> Move the kubectl binary to your PATH.
> Verify the installation of kubectl.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Set up a new Kubernetes cluster using Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create open-webui-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Create a GKE cluster with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your local environment to connect to the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials open-webui-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Fetch the cluster credentials.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster.

steps.title: Install Open WebUI
steps.description:
Deploy Open WebUI on your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add open-webui https://charts.openwebui.com

helm install open-webui open-webui/open-webui --version 0.8.9 --namespace open-webui --create-namespace

```

> Add the Open WebUI Helm repository.
> Install Open WebUI with the specified version.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for your Open WebUI deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment open-webui --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10 --namespace open-webui

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/open-webui/open-webui/main/monitoring/monitoring.yaml --namespace open-webui

```

> Set up horizontal pod autoscaling.
> Deploy monitoring resources.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Open WebUI
uninstall.description:
Remove Open WebUI from your cluster. This will delete all associated resources and may result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall open-webui --namespace open-webui

gcloud container clusters delete open-webui-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Uninstall the Open WebUI Helm release.
> Delete the GKE cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Open WebUI
upgrade.description:
Backup your current deployment, upgrade to the latest version, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get all --namespace open-webui -o yaml > open-webui-backup.yaml

helm upgrade open-webui open-webui/open-webui --version 0.8.9 --namespace open-webui

kubectl get pods --namespace open-webui

```

> Backup the current deployment.
> Upgrade Open WebUI to the latest version.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all --namespace open-webui -o yaml > open-webui-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error from server (NotFound): deployments.apps "open-webui" not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Open WebUI deployment may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Check the Helm installation logs with 'helm list --namespace open-webui' and ensure the namespace is correct.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image "openwebui/open-webui:v0.8.9": Error response from daemon: pull access denied'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The image may not be available or the repository is private.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and ensure you have access to the repository.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources for pod'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or resize the existing nodes in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl get nodes returns 'No resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster may not be properly set up or connected.

**Fix:**
Ensure you have the correct kubeconfig and that the cluster is running.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Ollama models, improved API integration features, and enhanced user permissions management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' when running 'kubectl get pods --namespace open-webui'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
